topic: Coupling dichotomy and sharp non-rectangular identified set for offline contextual-bandit policy value V(pi) under JOINT misspecification: a propensity Marginal-Sensitivity-Model budget Gamma together with a structural epsilon-tube (fixed center law F0, fixed metric, stated support regularity) on the conditional potential-outcome law that the MSM clips.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp coupled upper and lower endpoint for offline contextual-bandit policy value under a propensity MSM budget Gamma and a fixed-center conditional-quantile epsilon-tube, and exactly when is Frauen-style rectangular widening strictly conservative?

source_refs.claim: The anchor paper gives sharp and efficient MSM off-policy bounds under unobserved confounding, but does not add a structural fixed-center outcome-law tube to the same latent law.
source_refs.author: Hess, Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel

source_refs.claim: Sharp MSM/IPW sensitivity bounds are characterized through quantile-balancing geometry, supplying the active-rank clipping primitive but not the joint policy-value tube endpoint.
source_refs.author: Dorn and Guo

source_refs.claim: Enhanced MSM work adds outcome-side sensitivity restrictions to treatment-probability restrictions, showing joint constraints matter but not resolving the fixed-center quantile-tube policy-value endpoint.
source_refs.author: Zhang, Xu, and Tan

source_refs.excerpt: neither states the paired propensity-divergence plus nominal arm-mean sup-norm tube
the non-rectangular no-go refutes a generic rectangular post-processing port

open_problems.why_unsolved: The closest published off-policy paper supplies the MSM-only sharp endpoint and efficient one-step machinery, while the prior CausalSmith v1 run found that a generic joint-misspecification rectangle is not field-tier unless it targets a named estimator/workflow. The missing obstacle is the same latent conditional potential-outcome law having to satisfy both the MSM clipping extremizer and the fixed-center quantile tube; marginally sharp MSM and tube endpoints need not be jointly attainable.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would be a closed-form support-function formula for the sharp joint endpoint, together with a nonnegative rectangularity gap Delta_rect(Gamma, epsilon) and an equality/strict-positivity region stated in terms of the active MSM clip, tube radius, support headroom, and policy weights.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the named endpoint whose sharp off-policy estimator is being corrected, so its motivation arc should be mirrored before introducing the joint structural tube.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: offline policy value under hidden confounding -> MSM gives sharp efficient endpoint -> adding a fixed-center outcome-law tube should not be handled by rectangular widening -> derive the coupled endpoint and gap

method_exemplars.technique: Use bounded-likelihood-ratio/quantile-balancing extremizers and rearrangement to reduce the sharp bound to tail-rank clipping.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The coupled endpoint is still an MSM sharp-bound problem, but the quantile tube makes the active tail rank share a finite perturbation budget.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt simultaneous treatment-probability and outcome-side sensitivity restrictions as an intersection of constraints on one latent law.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the closest paper-level signal that treatment and outcome sensitivity restrictions can jointly tighten bounds beyond one-source envelopes.

method_exemplars.technique: Start from the sharp off-policy one-step estimator and replace the MSM endpoint map by the coupled endpoint map.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target estimator corrects exactly this paper's sharp MSM endpoint rather than building a new OPE estimator from scratch.

open_problems.open_problem: Can one characterize the dichotomy between additive conditional-mean tubes, which decouple from MSM clipping, and conditional-quantile tubes, which couple with the active MSM clip and make the joint identified set non-rectangular?

source_refs.claim: Sharp mean-effect sensitivity bounds under marginal sensitivity show how mean-function targets can be handled, but do not settle process-level quantile-law compatibility.
source_refs.author: Yadlowsky, Namkoong, Basu, Duchi, and Tian

source_refs.claim: The quantile-balancing approach shows rank-dependent active sets for sharp MSM bounds, making quantile tubes structurally different from scalar mean allowances.
source_refs.author: Dorn and Guo

source_refs.excerpt: Replace or supplement U_rect with a rectangular envelope derived from a one-source bound
a curve drawn by stacking sharp pointwise intervals may not itself correspond to any single latent law

open_problems.why_unsolved: The prior v1 proposal used a sup-norm arm-mean tube and reviewers asked for a better rectangular comparison, while the rejected QTE proposal recorded a process-compatibility obstruction for stacking pointwise quantile bounds. The unresolved distinction is structural: a scalar additive mean slack can be allocated independently of the MSM rank extremizer, but a quantile-function tube moves mass subject to monotonicity, support, and the same active rank set selected by the MSM clip.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would state two matched identification results under the same observed law: a rectangular product/support-sum formula for an additive conditional-mean tube and a strict non-rectangular inclusion theorem for a conditional-quantile tube, with a shared binary or finite-grid witness showing the contrast.

writing_exemplar.why: Its framing naturally moves from MSM sharpness to the rank geometry that makes quantile restrictions different from mean restrictions.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: MSM sensitivity gives sharp rank-clipping bounds -> mean targets collapse to scalar endpoints -> quantile/process targets impose compatibility across ranks -> identify when the joint set decouples or couples

method_exemplars.technique: Use sharp marginal-sensitivity mean-bound arguments as the decoupled scalar benchmark.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The mean-tube half of the dichotomy should reduce to a scalar mean sensitivity support function rather than a process feasibility problem.

method_exemplars.technique: Reuse finite-support quantile-cell compatibility constraints for a single latent arm law rather than independent pointwise endpoint choices.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The quantile-tube half is a law/process compatibility question, exactly the pathology flagged by the rejected QTE angle.

method_exemplars.technique: Port quantile-balancing empirical analogs for the active-rank quantities after the population dichotomy is established.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The estimator for the coupled branch needs the same rank-weight objects that quantile balancing estimates.

open_problems.open_problem: What support, density, and active-threshold regularity assumptions are sufficient and close to necessary for sharp quantile-tube coupling, and what parts of the endpoint formula survive atoms, flat CDF pieces, or support-boundary contact?

source_refs.claim: Efficient QTE estimation under selection on observables highlights the regular quantile/density conditions needed for smooth distributional targets.
source_refs.author: Firpo

source_refs.claim: Sharp distributional and quantile bounds under limited information make endpoint and inverse-CDF regularity a first-order issue for partial identification.
source_refs.author: Fan and Park

source_refs.excerpt: requires a careful treatment of atoms, flat CDF pieces, and support endpoints
continuous and strictly increasing on the relevant quantile interval

open_problems.why_unsolved: The quantile-tube endpoint is only well-behaved when the active MSM clip threshold and the tube boundary are interior and locally smooth. Prior QTE scouting explicitly deferred atoms, flat CDF pieces, and support endpoints, but the present joint problem makes those edge cases load-bearing because the rectangularity gap can vanish or become nondifferentiable exactly when the active tail touches a boundary.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would be a regime statement partitioning the endpoint formula into regular interior, atom/flat-CDF, and support-boundary cases, with explicit assumptions under which the sharpness construction attains the endpoint and the Delta_rect positive-region statement remains valid.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames distribution and quantile bounds as inverse-CDF objects whose endpoint behavior must be handled explicitly.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: partial information yields distribution bounds -> quantile bounds require inversion and endpoint conventions -> structural quantile tubes add active-rank thresholds -> state the regular and nonregular regimes for sharp coupling

method_exemplars.technique: Use quantile functional differentiability under positive density and interior quantile indices.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The EIF and uniform inference rung for the coupled endpoint needs the same local inverse-CDF smoothness.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt sharp distribution-to-quantile inversion with explicit limited-information endpoint cases.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The regularity problem is not just estimation; it determines which inverse-CDF bound is the sharp identified endpoint.

method_exemplars.technique: Reuse the finite-Gamma endpoint-boundary atlas and strictness clauses as local cases for the joint tube problem.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The same atoms/flat/support caveats become the cases where the coupling threshold kink may fail regularity.

open_problems.open_problem: Can the coupled sharp endpoint and rectangularity gap be estimated by a Neyman-orthogonal one-step procedure with valid uniform inference away from the coupling-threshold kink and appropriate nonregular inference at the kink?

source_refs.claim: Double/debiased ML supplies Neyman-orthogonal cross-fitting for smooth causal functionals, but not the nonsmooth coupled endpoint map.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, and Robins

source_refs.claim: Policy learning with observational data uses doubly robust scores for causal policy values, providing a policy-facing inference template but not partial-ID kink handling.
source_refs.author: Athey and Wager

source_refs.claim: The anchor gives the MSM-only efficient off-policy estimator that a coupled quantile-tube endpoint estimator would have to modify.
source_refs.author: Hess, Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel

source_refs.excerpt: estimation can be layered on later once the population bounds are known
does not claim new inference, confidence bands, efficient estimation

open_problems.why_unsolved: Population sharpness and regular estimation separate here. The coupled endpoint contains min/max and active-set maps at the tube/clip threshold, so a Frauen-style EIF is not automatically valid after substituting the coupled endpoint; the nuisance components include propensity, conditional quantile or density, support headroom, and the active threshold, and pathwise differentiability fails at the kink.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would give an EIF or directional derivative for the coupled endpoint, a cross-fit one-step estimator with rate conditions for all nuisance functions, a uniform CLT/coverage theorem on regions bounded away from the coupling kink, and a separate directional or conservative inference statement at the kink.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the estimator being corrected, and its inference narrative should be retained before explaining the new nonregular component.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: sharp MSM endpoint admits efficient off-policy estimation -> joint structural tube changes the endpoint map -> smooth regions keep an EIF correction -> kink regions require nonregular inference

method_exemplars.technique: Use Neyman-orthogonal scores, cross-fitting, and nuisance-rate product conditions for root-n inference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The estimator must be robust to ML estimation of logging, quantile/density, and center-law nuisance functions.

method_exemplars.technique: Modify the MSM sharp-bound EIF by adding derivative terms for the quantile-tube active-tail and headroom integrals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the exact baseline endpoint and one-step structure for the same off-policy value estimand.

method_exemplars.technique: Use inverse-CDF influence-function calculations under positive conditional density for quantile components.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The new endpoint's correction terms are functionals of conditional quantiles and active tail masses.

literature_map:
1. Tan (2006, JASA) supplies the propensity-odds marginal sensitivity primitive behind MSM bounds.
2. Kallus and Zhou (2018 NeurIPS; 2021 Management Science) bring unobserved-confounding uncertainty into robust policy learning, but not joint structural outcome-law tubes.
3. Dorn and Guo (2023, JASA/arXiv:2102.04543) show sharp MSM/IPW bounds through quantile balancing, the closest active-rank machinery.
4. Dorn, Guo, and Kallus (JASA/arXiv:2112.11449) add doubly valid and doubly sharp sensitivity inference, but for standard causal sensitivity targets rather than fixed-center quantile tubes.
5. Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel (arXiv:2305.16988) generalize sharp causal sensitivity, but do not isolate the mean-vs-quantile tube rectangularity dichotomy.
6. Hess, Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel (arXiv:2502.13022; ICLR 2026 per topic) are the named anchor for sharp efficient MSM off-policy estimation.
7. Zhang, Xu, and Tan (2025, arXiv:2504.08301) explicitly combine treatment and outcome sensitivity constraints, confirming the importance of intersections of restrictions.
8. Yadlowsky et al. (2022, Annals of Statistics) and related MSM mean-effect papers explain why mean targets can be scalar, but not why quantile-process targets couple.
9. Firpo (2007, Econometrica) supplies the point-identified QTE efficiency template and regular inverse-CDF conditions.
10. Fan and Park (2010, Econometric Theory) provide distribution/quantile partial-ID endpoint logic under limited information.
11. Chernozhukov et al. (2018, Econometrics Journal) provide DML orthogonality and cross-fitting tools for the estimator rung.
12. Athey and Wager (2021, Econometrica) provide the policy-learning/value-score template for causal policy functionals.
13. The main literature tension is that marginally sharp bounds are known for MSM or outcome restrictions separately, while the intersection over one latent conditional law can be strictly smaller than their rectangular product.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Mined the adjacent banked run pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1, including README, proposal, derivation note, reviews, and pipeline metadata.
2. v1 reached a mathematically plausible finite-grid joint program but was downgraded because its rectangular no-go targeted a generic shortcut rather than a named published estimator/workflow.
3. v1 reviewer JSONs repeatedly requested a concrete non-rectangular witness, a better U_rect definition, full factual reward-cell matching, and named comparators including Hess/Frauen and Kallus-Zhou.
4. Mined failed pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1 angle-rejected proposals; these flag process-level quantile compatibility and warn that stacked pointwise sharp quantile intervals may not correspond to one latent law.
5. The QTE angle1 source explicitly deferred atoms, flat CDF pieces, support endpoints, confidence bands, and efficient estimation.
6. Mined pid_policy_regret_ch_sensitivity_v1 as an adjacent policy-learning comparator source naming Hess/Frauen-style sharp off-policy work.
7. The provided study-pipeline gaps were mostly LATE, Manski, and interval-regression topics; they were treated as augmenting candidates but not emitted because they do not survive topic alignment with the offline-policy joint-misspecification anchor.
